Table of contents

  • Setup
  • Read in data
  • GPS Adherence for next 28 days
    • Update study end dates
  • Heat map GPS plot data
    • Plot next 28-day adherence

GPS EDA

Author

Kendra wyant

Published

February 23, 2026

Setup

Read in data

Code 
gps <- read_csv(here::here(path_shared, "gps_counts.csv"),
                show_col_types = FALSE) |> 
  rename(study_start = onboarding)

study_dates <- read_csv(here::here(path_shared, "study_dates_gps.csv"), 
                show_col_types = FALSE)

ema <- read_csv(here::here(path_shared, "survey_daily.csv"), 
                show_col_types = FALSE) |> 
  mutate(start_date = with_tz(start_date,tzone = "America/Chicago"))

contact_log <- read_csv(here::here(path_shared, "contact_log.csv"), 
                show_col_types = FALSE) |> 
  select(subid, study_start = onboarding, study_end = study_termination) |> 
  filter(subid %in% gps$subid) |> 
  filter(subid %in% ema$subid) |> 
  mutate(study_start = as_date(study_start, format = "%m/%d/%Y"),
         study_end = as_date(study_end, format = "%m/%d/%Y")) |> 
  filter(!subid %in% study_dates$subid) |> 
  left_join(ema |> 
              filter(!subid %in% study_dates$subid) |> 
              group_by(subid) |> 
              arrange(desc(start_date)) |> 
              slice_head(n = 1) |> 
              ungroup() |> 
              select(subid, ema_end = start_date),
            by = "subid")

study_dates <- study_dates |> 
  bind_rows(contact_log)

monthly <- read_csv(here::here(path_shared, "survey_monthly.csv"), 
                show_col_types = FALSE) |> 
  suppressWarnings()

context <- read_csv(here::here(path_shared, "gps_context.csv"), 
                show_col_types = FALSE)

GPS Adherence for next 28 days

Create days on study tibble

  • End 28 days before study end date

  • Get all study days for each subid

# function to map over
get_study_days <- function(the_subid, dates) {
  study_start <- dates |>
    filter(subid == the_subid) |>
    pull(study_start)
  study_end <- dates |>
    filter(subid == the_subid) |>
    mutate(ema_end = date(ema_end),
           study_end = pmin(study_end, ema_end, na.rm = TRUE)) |>
    pull(study_end)
  if(study_end - study_start > 28) {
    study_days <- tibble(subid = the_subid,
                       study_day = seq(study_start,
                                       study_end - days(28),
                                       by = "day"))
    return(study_days)
  }
}

sub_days <- study_dates$subid |>
  map_dfr(~get_study_days(.x, study_dates))

Starting with 88,870 days

nrow(sub_days)
[1] 88870

Add 28-day periods with 0 EMAs back in

gps <- gps |>
  rename(study_day = date) |> 
  right_join(sub_days, by = c("subid", "study_day")) |>
  mutate(n = if_else(is.na(n), 0, n)) |>
  arrange(subid, study_day)

Determine cut point for gps points per day

Calculate number of days with at least 1 gps point over next 28 days for each subid and study_day in sub_days

gps_count <- sub_days |>
  left_join(gps |>
              filter(n > 0) |> 
              rename(gps_day = study_day),
            by = c("subid"),
            relationship = "many-to-many") |> 
  arrange(subid, study_day) |>
  filter(gps_day > study_day & gps_day <= study_day + 28) |> 
  group_by(subid, study_day) |>
  summarise(n_gps_days = n(),
            .groups = "drop")

Calculate adherence for 28 day windows

gps_count <- gps_count |>
  mutate(n_days = 28,
         adherence = n_gps_days/n_days)

Add day number indicator

gps_count <- gps_count |>
  group_by(subid) |>
  mutate(day_num = row_number()) |>
  ungroup()

Update study end dates

  • 353 subids have gps data
  • 351 subids provided at least one month of data (without compliance threshold)
  • 303 subids with one month data after compliance threshold
  • 298 completed monthly survey and provided context data
length(unique(gps$subid))
[1] 353
length(unique(gps_count$subid))
[1] 351

Add new study end column that will be updated

study_dates <- study_dates |> 
  mutate(study_end_ad = study_end)

Set compliance threshold for discontinuing.

  1. Any time adherence over the next 28 days falls below .33 discontinuation will be considered.

  2. If adherence is below .33 for the majority of remaining days on study (< .75) they will be dropped.

  3. If adherence is below .33 for less than the majority of remaining days on study (>= .75) they will be retained.

  4. Discontinuation is considered every time adherence falls below .33

for (sub in unique(gps_count$subid)) {
  study_days <- pull(subset(gps_count, subid == sub), study_day)
  adherence <- pull(subset(gps_count, subid == sub), adherence)
  
  for (i in seq_along(adherence)) {
    
    if(adherence[i] < .33) {
      
      prop_g_33 <- mean(adherence[i:length(adherence)] > .33)
                      
      if(prop_g_33 < .75) {
        study_dates <- study_dates |> 
          mutate(study_end_ad = if_else(subid == sub, study_days[i], study_end_ad))
        break
      }
    }
  }
}

337 subids have updated study_end dates

study_dates |> 
  filter(study_end != study_end_ad) |> 
  glimpse()
Rows: 337
Columns: 5
$ subid        <dbl> 1006, 1009, 1010, 1012, 1014, 1017, 1020, 1022, 1023, 102…
$ study_start  <date> 2021-04-21, 2021-05-03, 2021-08-25, 2021-09-15, 2021-10-…
$ study_end    <date> 2022-04-29, 2022-05-09, 2022-05-17, 2022-10-05, 2022-11-…
$ ema_end      <dttm> 2022-04-25 03:49:24, 2022-05-09 23:01:30, 2022-04-06 03:…
$ study_end_ad <date> 2022-03-19, 2022-04-02, 2022-02-28, 2022-08-29, 2022-09-…

Heat map GPS plot data

Days in a week with more than 2 GPS points

Code 
gps_heat <- gps |> 
  rename(date = study_day) |> 
  left_join(study_dates |> select(subid, ema_end, study_end_ad),
            by = "subid") |> 
  mutate(study_end_ad = if_else(is.na(study_end_ad), ema_end, study_end_ad)) |> 
  group_by(subid) |> 
  filter(date <= study_end_ad) |> 
  ungroup() |> 
  filter(n > 0)

gps_heat_count <- gps_heat |>
  select(subid) |>
  distinct() |>
  left_join(gps_heat |>
  group_by(subid) |>
  filter(date > study_start & date < study_start + days(7)) |>
  count() |>
  mutate(week = 1) |>
  ungroup(), by = "subid") |>
  bind_rows(gps_heat |>
  group_by(subid) |>
  filter(date >= study_start + days(7) & date < study_start + days(14)) |>
  count() |>
  mutate(week = 2) |>
  ungroup()) |>
  bind_rows(gps_heat |>
  group_by(subid) |>
  filter(date >= study_start + days(14) & date < study_start + days(21)) |>
  count() |>
  mutate(week = 3) |>
  ungroup()) |>
  bind_rows(gps_heat |>
  group_by(subid) |>
  filter(date >= study_start + days(21) & date < study_start + days(28)) |>
  count() |>
  mutate(week = 4) |>
  ungroup()) |>

  group_by(subid) |>

  bind_rows(gps_heat |>
  group_by(subid) |>
  filter(date >= study_start + days(413) & date < study_start + days(420)) |>
  count() |>
  mutate(week = 60) |>
  ungroup()) |>
  bind_rows(gps_heat |>
  group_by(subid) |>
  filter(date >= study_start + days(420) & date < study_start + days(427)) |>
  count() |>
  mutate(week = 61) |>
  ungroup()) |>
  bind_rows(gps_heat |>
  group_by(subid) |>
  filter(date >= study_start + days(427) & date < study_start + days(434)) |>
  count() |>
  mutate(week = 62) |>
  ungroup()) |>
  bind_rows(gps_heat |>
  group_by(subid) |>
  filter(date >= study_start + days(434) & date < study_start + days(441)) |>
  count() |>
  mutate(week = 63) |>
  ungroup()) |>
  bind_rows(gps_heat |>
  group_by(subid) |>
  filter(date >= study_start + days(441) & date < study_start + days(448)) |>
  count() |>
  mutate(week = 64) |>
  ungroup())  |>
  bind_rows(gps_heat |>
  group_by(subid) |>
  filter(date >= study_start + days(448) & date < study_start + days(455)) |>
  count() |>
  mutate(week = 65) |>
  ungroup())  |>
  bind_rows(gps_heat |>
  group_by(subid) |>
  filter(date >= study_start + days(455) & date < study_start + days(462)) |>
  count() |>
  mutate(week = 66) |>
  ungroup())  |>
  bind_rows(gps_heat |>
  group_by(subid) |>
  filter(date >= study_start + days(462) & date < study_start + days(469)) |>
  count() |>
  mutate(week = 67) |>
  ungroup())  |>
  bind_rows(gps_heat |>
  group_by(subid) |>
  filter(date >= study_start + days(469) & date < study_start + days(476)) |>
  count() |>
  mutate(week = 68) |>
  ungroup())  |>
  bind_rows(gps_heat |>
  group_by(subid) |>
  filter(date >= study_start + days(476) & date < study_start + days(483)) |>
  count() |>
  mutate(week = 69) |>
  ungroup())  |>
  bind_rows(gps_heat |>
  group_by(subid) |>
  filter(date >= study_start + days(483) & date < study_start + days(490)) |>
  count() |>
  mutate(week = 70) |>
  ungroup())

gps_heat_count <- gps_heat_count |>
  mutate(week = if_else(is.na(week), 1, week))
Code 
get_study_days <- function(the_subid, dates) {
  study_start <- dates |>  
    filter(subid == the_subid) |>  
    pull(study_start)
  study_end <- dates |>  
    filter(subid == the_subid) |> 
    mutate(ema_end = date(ema_end),
           study_end = pmin(study_end_ad, ema_end, na.rm = TRUE)) |> 
    pull(study_end)
  if(study_end - study_start > 28) {
    study_days <- tibble(subid = the_subid, 
                       study_day = seq(study_start, 
                                       study_end - days(28), 
                                       by = "day")) 
    return(study_days)
  }
}

sub_days <- study_dates$subid |> 
  map_dfr(~get_study_days(.x, study_dates))

Started with 88,870 days, down to 65,261 days with compliance (compared to 78,691 ema days)

nrow(sub_days)
[1] 65261
gps_count <- sub_days |>
  left_join(gps |>
              filter(n > 0) |> 
              rename(gps_day = study_day),
            by = c("subid"),
            relationship = "many-to-many") |> 
  arrange(subid, study_day) |>
  filter(gps_day > study_day & gps_day <= study_day + 28) |> 
  group_by(subid, study_day) |>
  summarise(n_gps_days = n(),
            .groups = "drop")

gps_count <- gps_count |>
  right_join(sub_days, by = c("subid", "study_day")) |>
  mutate(n_gps_days = if_else(is.na(n_gps_days), 0, n_gps_days)) |>
  arrange(subid, study_day)


gps_count <- gps_count |> 
  mutate(n_days = 28,
         adherence = n_gps_days/n_days)

gps_count <- gps_count |> 
  group_by(subid) |> 
  mutate(day_num = row_number()) |> 
  ungroup()

303 subids with 28 days of data after compliance threshold applied

length(unique(gps_count$subid))
[1] 303

298 completed a monthly survey
298 provided gps context

monthly |> 
  filter(subid %in% gps_count$subid) |> 
  pull(subid) |> 
  unique() |> 
  length()
[1] 298
context |> 
  filter(subid %in% gps_count$subid) |> 
  pull(subid) |> 
  unique() |> 
  length()
[1] 298

Plot next 28-day adherence

subids <- unique(gps_count$subid) 

# split into groups of 10 plotting
subid_groups <- split(subids, ceiling(seq_along(subids) / 10))

map(subid_groups, function(ids) {
  plot_1 <- gps_count |>
    filter(subid %in% ids) |>
    ggplot(aes(x = day_num, y = adherence,
               group = subid, color = factor(subid))) +
    geom_line() +
    scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 364, 28), limits = c(0, 372)) +
    scale_y_continuous(breaks = c(.25, .5, .75, 1.0), limits = c(0, 1)) +
    labs(color = NULL) +
    geom_hline(aes(yintercept = .50), linetype = "dashed") +
    geom_hline(aes(yintercept = .33), linetype = "dotted") +
    theme_classic()

  plot_2 <- gps_heat_count |>
    filter(subid %in% ids) |>
    mutate(subid = factor(subid, levels = rev(ids))) |>
    ggplot(aes(x = week, y = subid, fill = n)) +
    geom_tile() +
    scale_fill_viridis_c(option = "C",
                         name = NULL,
                         na.value = "white") +
    scale_x_continuous(
      breaks = c(1, 4, 8, 12, 16, 20, 24, 28, 32, 36, 40, 44, 48, 52, 56,
                 60, 64),
      limits = c(-1, 65),
      expand = c(0, 0)
    ) +
    labs(x = "Week number", y = "subid") +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(panel.grid = element_blank()) +
    geom_vline(xintercept = 4) +
    geom_vline(xintercept = 52) +
    theme(legend.position = "bottom")

  plot_1 + plot_2
})
